info.title: Holonet Objects API
info.description:
The Dreamteam CRM API lets you programmatically access and manage your CRM data — contacts, companies, deals, users, and pipelines. All endpoints follow REST conventions and return JSON.

## Authentication
All requests require an OAuth2 bearer token. Your Bearer token identifies your organization — no tenant header or subdomain needed. Tokens do not expire automatically — they remain valid until you rotate them.

### Getting your API token
1. Click your **profile icon** (top-right corner)
2. Click **View Profile**
3. Scroll to the **API Token** section at the bottom

The panel shows three actions on the right of the masked token field:

- 👁 **Reveal Token** — show the token in plaintext (default is masked with dots)
- 📋 **Copy Token** — copy the token to your clipboard
- 🔄 **Reset Token** — rotate the token. The previous token is invalidated immediately, so any integrations using it will start returning `401 Unauthorized` until you update them with the new token.

![API Token panel with Reveal, Copy, and Reset icons](images/api-token.png)

Rotate your token immediately if you suspect a security breach.

## Content-Type
- `application/json` for POST and PUT
- `application/merge-patch+json` for PATCH

## Naming
Request and response bodies are **snake_case**. The Filter endpoint additionally accepts camelCase field names (`filterGroups`/`fieldPath`) — but `fieldPath` *values* are always snake_case attribute names (e.g. `owner_id`).

## Timestamps
All timestamps in API responses are in **UTC** using ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.ffffffZ`. The trailing `Z` indicates UTC. Use the same format when sending date values in requests (e.g. `expected_close_date`).

## Associations
Associations live in their own block — never inside `properties`. Embed up to 100 per type via `?associations=` query parameter. Use the dedicated Associations endpoints to page beyond that. For Create record, pass an optional `associations: [{id, kind}]` array to link related records at creation time.

tags.name: Records
tags.description: Create, read, update, and filter operations over any object type

tags.name: Schema
tags.description: Object type metadata, field definitions, and association definitions

get.description: Returns a page of records the caller is authorized to view. RBAC row-level scoped.

post.description:
Creates one record. Fields go inside `properties`. Associations are optional — pass an array of `{id, kind}` to link related records at creation time. Use the Schema endpoint under Getting Started to see valid fields for each object type.

**Note on naming.** When creating associations, use `kind` with the simple object type name (e.g. `"kind": "company"`). In GET responses, embedded associations return `type` with the full association name (e.g. `"type": "company_deal_assoc"`). These refer to the same link — the keys and values are just named differently in requests vs responses.

get.description:
Fetch one record. Use `?associations=contact&associations=company` to embed linked records (capped at 100 per type). Supported association types: `contact`, `company`, `deal`, `note`.

To fetch a note's content, call this same endpoint with `object=note` — notes are first-class records at `/api/v2/objects/note/records/{noteId}`.

put.description: Replaces the record's properties with the provided fields. **All required fields must be included** — omitted required fields trigger a `VALIDATION_FAILED / REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING` error. Non-required fields that are omitted are set to `null` (see the sample response — `owner_id` was omitted from the request and appears as `null` in the response). Use PATCH instead if you only need to update a few fields.

200.description: Record replaced. Note that fields omitted from the request are cleared — see the example.

patch.description: Merges the provided fields into the existing record. Omitted fields are left unchanged. Set a field to `null` to explicitly clear it. Content-Type must be `application/merge-patch+json`.

post.description: Compound filtering. Filter groups are OR'd; filters within a group are AND'd. The request body accepts BOTH camelCase (`filterGroups` / `fieldPath`) and snake_case (`filter_groups` / `field_path`) field names — `field_path` *values* are always snake_case attribute names (e.g. `owner_id`). For the 2-request (vs N+1) hydrate pattern, use `IN` against the `id` attribute to resolve associated IDs collected from an embedded record page.

Create or update multiple records in one call. Set `operation` to `create` or `update`. Maximum **100 records** per batch.

**Every batch request must include `"all_or_none": false`.** This is the only supported value — transactional mode (`true`) is not available. Valid records are committed and per-record errors are reported in `results`.

**Important:** Batch create passes fields directly in each record object — there is no `properties` wrapper like single Create uses. Compare: single Create sends `{ "properties": { "first_name": "..." } }`; batch Create sends `{ "first_name": "..." }` directly.

For update, include the id field along with fields to change; `id_property` (default `id`) selects the match key.

get.description: Page through a record's links of one associated object type. Returns raw `{id, type}` link entries (no record data), ordered `created_at,id`. Use this to page past the 100-entry embed cap on record responses. **Link writes (create/update/delete) are not implemented yet.**

Returns the full field schema for an object type. **Always call this first** before creating, updating, or filtering records — it tells you the valid field names, types, and constraints (required, unique, read-only) for each object.
RBAC-scoped page of users in the caller's organization. Returns the safe-field projection only — token/password/OTP/lockout/api_key columns are never returned.
Safe-field projection only — token/password/OTP/lockout/api_key columns are never returned.

results.name: Default Pipeline
results.description: Standard deal flow pipeline with common stages.

results.name: Deal Sales Pipeline
results.description: Pipeline for managing standard deal opportunities.

example.name: Default Pipeline
example.description: Standard deal flow pipeline with common stages.

get.description: Returns the association definitions a type can participate in. Use the optional `target_type` query parameter to filter to definitions linking to a specific target. Reads through to the association catalog — useful for discovering valid `kind`/`type` before reading links.

parameters.name: target_type
parameters.description: Optional — restrict to definitions linking to this target object type

bearerAuth.description: OAuth2 bearer token. Organization resolved from `orgId` claim.

PageSize.name: page_size
PageSize.description: Page size. Default 20. Max 100 — higher values are silently capped at 100 (no error).

Associations.name: associations
Associations.description: Object types to embed (repeatable, capped at 100 per type)

associations.description: Optional. Link related records at creation time.

all_or_none.description: **Required.** Must be `false`. Only partial-success mode is supported — valid records are committed and per-record failures appear in `results`. Transactional mode (`true`) is not available.

id_property.description: The field used to match records for updates. Currently only `id` is supported. Required for `update` operations.

records.description: For `create`, pass field values directly (no `properties` wrapper — unlike single Create). For `update`, include the ID field along with fields to change.

BatchRequest.description: Create or update multiple records in a single request. **You must include `all_or_none: false` in every batch request** — it is the only supported value.

UserSafe.description: User identity. Sensitive fields (token, password, api_key) are never returned.

stage_category.description: e.g. Early Stage, Mid-Level Stage, Closed Won

is_closed.description: true for terminal stages (Closed Won / Closed Lost)

Pipeline.description: Pipeline. List responses omit `stages`; Get by ID returns the full stages array.

ObjectTypeDescriptor.description: Effective (system + tenant-override) schema for the type. `version` increments on schema change.

RecordPage.description: Paginated record list. When `?associations=` is passed, each record includes an `associations` block.

BadRequest.description: Validation error — see the `errors` array for specifics.

errors.message: Value 'umashankar@acme.com' already exists for this contact (conflicting record ID: 1012888).

NotFound.description: Resource not found — the record ID does not exist for the given object type, or the object type slug itself is invalid (e.g. `contacts` instead of `contact`).

value.message: Custom object data not found with id: 24 and objectName: contact

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized — the Bearer token is invalid, missing, or has been regenerated.
